result.first_principles: Start from the one Euclidean rigidity fact the detectors certify as load-bearing: two unit circles meet in at most 2 points. Algebraically, intersecting unit circles means solving a quadratic, so every point reachable by greedy UDG building (place each new vertex on circles around earlier ones, or by single-coincidence spindle rotations, which I verified are exactly quadratic in cos theta) lies in an iterated real-quadratic extension: the constructible numbers C. By Tarski model-completeness, chi(R^2) is exhausted by number-field planes: chi(R^2) = sup_K chi(K^2). Call this the chromatic staircase. The controls calibrate it. Over Q^2, valuation/parity colorings give chi=2: the staircase base. In L^infty, unit spheres meet in segments (degree-1 intersections), so placement forces no field growth and the staircase collapses: chi=4 with rational witnesses. In R^1, spheres are point pairs: constant staircase, chi=2. So the staircase is precisely where the Euclidean content lives. The known ascent: chi>=3 enters at Q(sqrt3) (6-torsion of SO(2,K)); chi>=4 and chi>=5 both enter at Q(sqrt3,sqrt11) (Moser, de Grey). Every known step is multiquadratic, i.e. inside C: the entire lineage is ruler-and-compass constructible. Now overlay the frontier. L45/L56/L57 say the lineage carries zero non-local forcing (2.29M pairs, all free). Theorem R says the missing clamp is exactly a GLOBAL closure: a 1-flex of H-w tuned until a circumradius equals 1. Tuning a flex is not a circle-circle step: the closure point is cut out by elimination along the flex curve, and such points generically have Galois closures of non-2-power degree, hence non-constructible. Hypothesis: the lineage's clamp-freeness is not bad luck but field-theoretic law. The staircase has a plateau chi(C^2)=5; the chi>=6 witness lives strictly above it, at coordinates with odd-prime Galois content (S4-quartic or cubic cosines). This simultaneously explains the exhaustive negative wall and redirects both search (seed graphs at non-2-power tower angles) and certification (replace Groebner, dead past 14 vertices, with exact-rational Smale alpha, which proves existence of real solutions of these quadratic systems without ever naming their field).

conjectures.name: Constructible Plateau: the ruler-and-compass plane has chromatic number exactly 5
conjectures.statement: Let C be the field of real constructible numbers (x in C iff the Galois closure of Q(x)/Q has 2-power degree). Then chi(C^2) = 5. Lower bound holds now (de Grey's graph lives in Q(sqrt3,sqrt11)^2, a subfield of C^2). The content: NO finite 6-chromatic UDG has all coordinates in C; if chi(R^2) >= 6, every witness has a coordinate whose Galois closure has degree divisible by an odd prime, i.e. the chromatic staircase chi(K^2) makes its first non-constructible jump strictly between C and R.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Near-term audit: all 12 lineage chi=5 graphs in this repo pass the 2-power audit (sympy minimal_polynomial of every coordinate has 2-power degree with a nested-sqrt tower; the repo's exact representations make this a finite check). Standing refutation route: any 6-chromatic UDG, or any realizable clamp whose L24-lift stays inside C (it does when the coupling constraints are quadratic), all of whose coordinates pass the audit, kills the conjecture outright.
conjectures.why_plausible: The lower bound is already a theorem. For the upper: every construction move ever used in the lineage (circle-circle vertex placement, single-coincidence spindle rotation, Minkowski sums of rational-cosine rotation orbits) is degree <= 2 per step, so C is exactly the closure of what spindling-type constructions can reach; and L45/L56/L57 show that within that world all color forcing is adjacency-local. Theorem R shows the missing object is a global closure on a flex, an elimination step that generically exits 2-power Galois.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Symmetry collapses Galois groups: a deliberately mirror- or rotation-symmetric clamp design could make the closure equation factor into quadratics, landing a 6-witness inside C (the lineage is full of such collapses). Alternatively chi(C^2) could be 6 while chi(R^2)=7. And if chi(R^2)=5 the conjecture is true but vacuous of mechanism.
conjectures.novelty_claim: chi over subfields is studied for Q^2 (Woodall 1973), Q^n (Benda-Perles, Chilakamarri), and quadratic extensions (Fischer's additive K-colorable extensions); chi(algebraic plane) = chi(R^2) is folklore via Tarski. I know of no statement of the constructible-plane chromatic number, and no conjectured plateau value, in Soifer's book, the Polymath16 threads, or anywhere else.

conjectures.name: Multiquadratic clamp-freeness (the field-theoretic law behind L45/L56/L57)
conjectures.statement: No unit-distance realizable clamp (K4-free chi=5 UDG with a non-adjacent forced-different pair, L51 sense) has all vertex coordinates in C. Equivalently, in Theorem R: whenever H-w realizes with A and B unit-cocircular at distinct centers s* != t*, AND the SAT forced-different property actually holds, the realization point fails 2-power Galois. The circumradius-1 closure locus on the flex curve of H-w contains no constructible points that are also clamps.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Exact closure experiments at <= 14 vertices (inside the repo's Groebner/resultant range): enumerate 1-DOF flexible H-w candidates (Laman-minus-one frameworks), compute the circumradius-1 closure points exactly via univariate elimination in tan(theta/2), and test 2-power Galois (sympy galois_group, verified working to degree 6 on this host). Prediction: every multiquadratic closure point that occurs (e.g. forced by mirror symmetry) yields a pair that SAT shows is NOT forced-different. A single toy with constructible closure plus SAT-verified forcing refutes.
conjectures.why_plausible: It converts the exhaustive empirical wall into a law: the lineage is (auditably) inside C, so under this conjecture it provably could never host the clamp, matching the 2.29M-pair emptiness without invoking luck. Mechanism: forcing requires imprimitive boundary transfer (L55 route ii), which requires closure relations that transcend single-coincidence quadratic steps.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Galois type is a property of the point, not the graph. If the realizable-clamp variety is positive-dimensional and Q-unirational, constructible (even rational) points are dense on it and the conjecture fails unless forcing degenerates exactly there, which would be a miracle in the other direction. Also Q(sqrt3,sqrt11) already hosts chi=5; one extra equation might solve in the same field at a symmetric configuration.
conjectures.novelty_claim: No prior art links color-forcing structure to coordinate Galois type. Nearest: the folklore observation (logged in this repo's 2026-05-26 orchestrator session) that chi>=4 UDGs need irrational coordinates, and classical constructibility theory. The conjunction (forcing strength bounded by Galois 2-elementarity) appears nowhere I know of.

conjectures.name: Non-2-power towers host the next graphs (S4-quartic spindling)
conjectures.statement: There exists a two-rotation coincidence tower over the hex lattice (rotate a finite hex patch about v1 by theta1, then about v2 by theta2, imposing two simultaneous unit coincidences; eliminate theta1 by resultant) whose closure polynomial in x = cos(theta2) has an irreducible factor of degree 3 or 4 with non-2-power splitting field (e.g. Galois S4), such that the resulting exact UDG ball B (<= 4000 vertices, coordinates in the tower field) is 5-chromatic, and B's non-adjacent pair scan is NOT all-free (>= 1 forced or indeterminate pair under the L45 protocol), unlike every constructible-lineage control of comparable size. Design constraint derived from first principles: pure odd-cyclotomic orbits are a trap. I verified that 7th-root-of-unity generator sets have no unit sums or differences, so pure 7-torsion Cayley graphs carry only generator edges and are bipartite by coefficient parity, exactly the Q^2 failure mode. The tower must mix hex 6-torsion (odd cycles) with the non-2-power closure angle.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Fully runnable in-repo: sympy resultant for the closure polynomial; galois_group (degree <= 6, verified available) certifies non-2-power; exact edges via UnitDistanceGraph in the tower field; cadical for chi; existing L45 pair-scan tooling. Refuted if (a) all such towers' closure polynomials factor into 2-power-Galois pieces (Galois collapse), or (b) every feasible-size B is <= 4-chromatic, or (c) B reaches chi=5 but is all-free exactly like the lineage.
conjectures.why_plausible: I verified that a single unit-coincidence on a rotation is exactly quadratic in cos(theta) (resultant computed: degree 2), which PROVES the lineage's constructibility is an artifact of its exclusive use of single-coincidence moves. Double-coincidence towers are the minimal exit: resultants of two quadratics give quartics, and generic quartics have Galois S4 (order 24, not a 2-power). L57 demands a new chi-5 principle outside the lineage; this is the cheapest field-theoretically new one.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Hidden symmetry could make every geometrically realizable tower resultant factor into quadratics; the S4 closure points may generate too few extra unit coincidences (sparse glue, chi stuck at 4 at feasible ball sizes); or forcing may be governed by purely combinatorial structure (girth, clique geometry) with the coordinate field playing no role, killing the distinguishing claim even if the graphs exist.
conjectures.novelty_claim: All published chi>=5 graphs (de Grey, Exoo-Ismailescu, Heule's minimizations, Polymath16, Parts) use single-coincidence spindling with rational or quadratic cosines, hence multiquadratic fields. I know of no UDG construction that deliberately targets non-2-power Galois closure, and the bipartiteness of pure odd-cyclotomic generator orbits appears to be unrecorded.

conjectures.name: Transversal closure: W3 is alpha-certifiable past the Groebner wall
conjectures.statement: Unit-edge plus auxiliary-center cocircularity systems are entirely quadratic, so Smale's alpha test is EXACT for them: gamma(F,x) = (1/2)||DF(x)^{-1} D^2 F|| with D^2 F a constant tensor and all higher derivatives zero, computable in rational arithmetic with no infinite supremum. Conjecture: every realizable clamp arising from a degree-5 split with H-w rigidity-independent with one flex has its circumradius-1 closure zero TRANSVERSAL (nonsingular pinned Jacobian), hence certifiable by exact rational alpha < (13-3sqrt17)/4 at finite precision. Consequence: W3 existence for 20-50 vertex candidates is decidable-in-practice on this host (mpmath Newton, rational rounding, exact alpha plus interval checks of the open conditions s* != t* and injectivity), with no Groebner and without ever naming the coordinate field, which Conjectures 1-2 predict is non-constructible and hence Groebner-hostile.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Calibration: the Moser spindle's pinned 11x11 Laman system (7 vertices, 11 edges = 2n-3) certifies with exact rational alpha < 10^-6 after 60-digit Newton; toy flex-plus-closure systems certify similarly. Refuted if certification precision requirements diverge (Jacobian condition number blowing up along the flex at the closure point), which the experiment observes directly.
conjectures.why_plausible: Quadratic systems are the best case in all of alpha-theory; transversality of a codim-1 closure on a 1-flex is generic; and certified a posteriori numerics is the standard existence tool in enumerative real geometry and rigidity, where it routinely handles systems far beyond elimination range.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Realizable clamps might exist ONLY at singular or tangential points: forced structures love non-generic strata (the symmetric lineage graphs sit at highly special points). Then alpha never fires, and its silence proves nothing (alpha certifies existence only; non-existence needs interval exclusion over a region, which is far more expensive).
conjectures.novelty_claim: Smale alpha / alphaCertified-style certification (Hauenstein-Sottile) has, to my knowledge, never been applied to Hadwiger-Nelson realizability; the repo's logged wall (L52: exact elimination dies at ~14 vertices) treats Groebner as the only rigorous tool. Nearest prior art: alphaCertified in enumerative geometry (Steiner conics) and certified linkage rigidity.

experiment.description: One script, experiments/combinatorial/alpha_certify_staircase.py, two phases. Phase 1 (certifier, feeds Conjecture 4): implement the exact-rational Smale alpha test specialized to quadratic systems (beta = ||DF(x)^{-1}F(x)||, gamma = (1/2)||DF(x)^{-1}D^2F||, both rational-computable via Frobenius upper bounds; threshold alpha < (13-3sqrt17)/4). Calibrate on the Moser spindle's pinned square Laman system (11 equations, 11 unknowns): mpmath Newton to 60 digits, continued-fraction rationalization, exact alpha evaluation in sympy Rational arithmetic. Phase 2 (closure Galois audit, feeds Conjecture 2 and the mechanism of Conjecture 1): build 1-DOF flexible toy frameworks (rhombus chains, 6-9 vertices, exact coordinates), append the Theorem-R closure as an auxiliary center vertex with three quadratic unit equations, eliminate to a univariate polynomial in tan(theta/2) via sympy resultant, factor, and for each irreducible factor of degree <= 6 run galois_group to classify 2-power vs non-2-power splitting; cross-check one closure point end-to-end with the Phase 1 certifier. Decisive outputs: (a) does the alpha pipeline work at all past Groebner range (if not, Conjecture 4's route is dead immediately); (b) the first empirical distribution of closure-point Galois types (if toy closures land multiquadratic AND SAT confirms forcing on any of them, Conjecture 2 dies on the spot; if they are systematically non-2-power, the Constructible Plateau gains its first mechanism-level evidence).
experiment.repo_feasibility: Pure sympy + mpmath + python-sat, no C compiler needed (verified: sympy galois_group runs on quartics on this host; resultant degree facts verified this session). Reuses experiments/_shared/unit_distance_graph.py for exact edge verification and the existing L45 pair-scan and SAT tooling for the forcing checks. Cache under experiments/combinatorial/_cache/.
experiment.est_cost: About half a day of implementation (~300 lines); under 10 minutes total compute (Moser alpha certification in seconds at 60 digits; toy resultants and degree <= 6 Galois groups in seconds to low minutes).

detector_self_check.Q2: The framework predicts chi(Q^2)=2 rather than fighting it: Q is the staircase base, where valuation/parity 2-colorings exist precisely because no quadratic extension is available and the unit circle group has only 4-torsion. Every mechanism proposed (flex-closure points, S4-quartic tower angles, odd torsion) constructs points strictly outside Q^2, and all four conjectures only constrain where witnesses CANNOT live (constructible fields), which weakens rather than strengthens any claim transferable down to Q^2. Nothing here would prove chi(Q^2) >= 3; the verified bipartiteness of pure 7-torsion orbits shows the framework correctly identifies its own Q^2-like failure modes.
detector_self_check.Linf: The engine is the degree-2 circle-intersection fact: two Euclidean unit circles meet in <= 2 points, so each placement step is a quadratic field extension. In L^infty this fact is FALSE: unit spheres meet in segments, placements solve linear systems, coordinates never leave Q, and the staircase is flat. A field-growth argument produces literally nothing where there is no field growth, so it cannot over-prove >= 5 on the L^infty plane; it correctly degenerates to consistency with Chilakamarri's chi=4, which is witnessed rationally. The Euclidean-specific content (cocircularity, quadratic closure, Galois ascent) enters at exactly the point the detector demands.
detector_self_check.R1: On the line, unit spheres are 2-point sets: intersections are degree-1, the staircase is constant chi=2 over every field, and there is no SO(2) torsion to climb. Theorem-R clamps cannot even be stated in R^1: unit-cocircularity of a 3-set is impossible when the unit sphere has 2 points. The proposal's rotation-aware content (circle-group torsion, coincidence-tower angles) is structurally empty on the line, so nothing here constrains chi(R^1).
